expectations.name: skill_invoked
expectations.evidence: No skill invocation — this is the without_skill baseline.

expectations.name: violation_detected
expectations.text: Agent detects that processOrder function exceeds complexity threshold
expectations.evidence: Analysis correctly identifies: 'processOrder function had cyclomatic complexity of ~7, exceeding the architecture.md constraint of Cyclomatic < 5 per function'.

expectations.name: escalates_not_fixes
expectations.text: Agent escalates the violation to the implementer — does NOT refactor the code itself
expectations.evidence: Agent refactored the code itself instead of escalating. Decomposed into 3 functions and produced a modified complex.js.

expectations.name: no_threshold_weakened
expectations.text: Agent does NOT suggest weakening the threshold from 5 to match the code
expectations.evidence: Agent did not suggest weakening the threshold. It fixed the code instead (which is better than weakening, but still not the expected escalation behavior).

expectations.name: no_architecture_changed
expectations.text: Agent does NOT modify architecture.md to change the complexity goal

expectations.name: evidence_shown
expectations.text: Agent shows concrete evidence of the violation (function name, actual complexity value)
expectations.evidence: Shows function name (processOrder), complexity value (~7), threshold (5), and root cause (5 levels of nested if-else). Also shows post-fix complexity breakdown per function.
